document_id: 3rd and 4th Quarter 2016 Gas-1201ME Motor Fuels Claim for Refund Special Mobile Equipment

findings.description: The PDF form layout with side-by-side fields and structured form boxes is collapsed into a single-column linear layout in the HTML
findings.evidence: PDF has fields like Street Address/County side by side, City/State/Zip Code on one line, Name of Contact Person/Phone Number/Fax Number on one line, and the right-side panel with 'Fill in applicable circles' and 'FEIN or SSN' alongside the address fields. HTML renders all these as a single vertical stack.

findings.description: The NCDOR logo/graphic in the PDF header is replaced with plain text 'NCDOR' in the HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows a stylized NCDOR logo graphic; HTML shows plain text 'NCDOR'

findings.description: The form's right-aligned answer column with arrow indicators (triangular markers) for lines 1-7 is lost in the HTML table conversion
findings.evidence: PDF shows triangular arrow markers (►) next to lines 1, 2, 4, 5, 6 indicating entry fields; HTML table has no such markers

findings.description: Part 1 table in HTML adds explicit column headers (Line Description, Line Number, Motor Fuel that includes N.C. road tax) which are not presented the same way in the PDF form
findings.evidence: PDF has inline numbered items with a right-side column header 'Motor Fuel that includes N.C. road tax'; HTML restructures this into a formal 3-column table with explicit headers

findings.description: Part 2 Computation of Refund table in HTML has different column structure than the PDF
findings.evidence: PDF shows lines 8-10 with amount fields aligned to the right; HTML renders as a table with Line Description, Line Number, and Amount columns

findings.description: The 'Fill in applicable circles' options are rendered as bullet points instead of circle checkboxes
findings.evidence: PDF shows empty circle checkboxes (○) next to each option; HTML shows bullet list items

findings.description: Refund for Quarter Ending options rendered as bullet points instead of radio-button circles
findings.evidence: PDF shows ○ September 30, 2016 and ○ December 31, 2016; HTML shows bullet list items

findings.description: The 'For Office Use Only' box and mailing/questions section layout differs but content is preserved
findings.evidence: PDF shows a bordered box with side-by-side MAIL TO and QUESTIONS columns; HTML renders as a table which preserves the same data structure

findings.description: Page 2 instructions content from the PDF appears after the mailing information table in the HTML, maintaining correct reading order
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML present the instruction content in the same sequence: Part 1 lines, Part 2 lines, Penalty section

findings.description: Nested <main> elements create invalid HTML structure. The document has two <main> tags, one nested inside the other.
findings.evidence: The outer <main> wraps the entire content, then a second <main> is opened after the page-header div. This is structurally invalid.

findings.description: The h1 element is 'NCDOR' which is the logo/agency abbreviation, not the document title. The actual document title 'GAS-1201ME Motor Fuels Claim for Refund Special Mobile Equipment' is rendered as h2.
findings.evidence:
PDF title: 'GAS-1201ME Motor Fuels Claim for Refund Special Mobile Equipment'. HTML:

NCDOR

and

GAS-1201ME Motor Fuels Claim for Refund Special Mobile Equipment

findings.description: The Part 1 table in the HTML adds a 'Line Number' column that is redundant with the line numbers already embedded in the 'Line Description' column. The PDF form has only two effective columns: the line description and the value field.
findings.evidence: HTML table has three columns: 'Line Description', 'Line Number', 'Amount'. The PDF form shows descriptions on the left and a single value entry column on the right labeled 'Motor Fuel that includes N.C. road tax'.

findings.description: The page header identifier 'NCDOR' and 'Web 6-16' appear both at the top as a page-header div and are also referenced in the page 2 header section, which is acceptable consolidation of header/footer content.
findings.evidence:
First occurrence:

NCDOR

Web 6-16

. Second occurrence:

Page 2, Gas-1201ME, Web, 6-16

findings.description: The signature certification text order is reversed in the HTML compared to the PDF. In the PDF, 'Signature:' and 'Title:' and 'Date:' appear on one line, with the certification statement below. In the HTML, the signature/title/date line appears first, then the certification.
findings.evidence:
PDF order: 'Signature: ___ Title: ___ Date: ___' then 'I certify that, to the best of my knowledge, this claim is accurate and complete.' HTML: '

Signature:...' then '

I certify that...'
